response:
To find the probability that a student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A we define a new variable D as X_B minus X_A. Since both X_A and X_B are normally distributed the difference D is also normally distributed. The mean of D equals 75 minus 70 which is 5. The variance of D equals the sum of the variances of X_A and X_B which is 10 squared plus 12 squared or 100 plus 144 resulting in 244. The standard deviation of D is the square root of 244 which is approximately 15.62. We seek the probability that D is greater than zero. We calculate the z-score by taking 0 minus 5 and dividing by 15.62 to get approximately -0.32. The probability corresponding to a z-score greater than -0.32 is found by looking up the value in a standard normal table or using a calculator which yields approximately 0.6255.

Regarding the performance improvement we must solve for the time t where P_A(t) equals P_B(t). The equation is 50 times e to the power of 0.02t equals 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of negative 0.15 times the quantity t minus 5. This mathematical process requires numerical methods because the variables appear in both exponential and rational forms. An explanation of the solution shows that at t equals 10 the left side is approximately 61.07 while the right side is approximately 60.87. At t equals 11 the left side is approximately 62.31 while the right side is approximately 64.76. By interpolation the time when both groups have the same performance level is approximately 10.02 weeks. Nothing in this force of growth prevents the curves from intersecting at this specific point.
